blocks.content: The intensive training began in earnest.
Shirone focused on practicing Scatter Movement during practical sessions and studied the theory of Laser Guidance Technology during breaks.
Thanks to an hour of tutoring from Isabel each day, he was gradually getting a grasp on radio waves.
Flu would evaluate his daily progress after returning from her duties.
However, no matter how hard Shirone tried, Flu's strict evaluations never seemed to yield a passing grade.

blocks.speaker: Flu
blocks.content: Too slow. Again.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Gritting his teeth, Shirone cast Scatter Movement.
Each time, Flu would appear in front of him, gripping his shoulders with both hands.
Despite hoping for a lucky break, it seemed professional mages didn't make mistakes.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: How does she keep finding me? Can a human really react this quickly?
It was hard to believe it was just because Shirone was inexperienced; Flu's sensory perception was incredibly sharp.
Regardless, Shirone couldn't complain about being guided by such an excellent mage, and the days continued to accumulate.

blocks.speaker: Flu
blocks.content: Ugh!
With a dull thud, Shirone rolled on the floor, his back arching in pain and his face contorted in agony.
Focusing too much on photon output, he missed the landing point of his teleportation.

blocks.speaker: Flu
blocks.content: Again.
A brilliant flash exploded in the center of the training ground.
Photon output scattered in seven directions, and Shirone safely reached his destination. Flu's face was nowhere to be seen.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Got it! This time it worked!
It seemed Flu hadn't been able to track Scatter Movement. As expected, she hadn't moved from her original spot.

blocks.speaker: Flu
blocks.content: Again.
Panting, Shirone asked, 'Why? The Force was perfect, and you didn't catch me.'

blocks.speaker: Flu
blocks.content: Look where you're standing.
Shirone looked around his feet. The direction was correct, and there was nothing unusual.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What's wrong with here?

blocks.speaker: Flu
blocks.content: You only moved about 8 meters. Don't you usually set your teleportation distance to 10 meters?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Oh... How could he make such a basic mistake?

blocks.speaker: Flu
blocks.content: Listen. Your teleportation distance must never waver, no matter the situation. In real combat, hundreds of turns pass in an instant, and you have to navigate complex terrain. If your distance wavers, you die. That's why even high-level mages rarely change their set distance during battle.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Yes, I'm sorry.
Flu put her hands on her hips and exhaled sharply at his dejected response.
If your basics are weakening, it means you're becoming more technically proficient. But you can't let your guard down. All applications ultimately come from solid basics.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone clenched his fists and took her words to heart.
Basics, basics are everything.
The next day.
Shirone finally succeeded in Scatter Movement.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Ah... I finally did it.
He was so tense that he couldn't even fully appreciate the joy of success.

blocks.speaker: Flu
blocks.content: Now, let's get serious. Your own Scatter Movement.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Understanding Flu's meaning, Shirone quickly adjusted his expression.
Scatter Movement was a good spell on its own, but the reason he chose it from among many magic books was its potential to be converted into an attack spell.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Don't be nervous. Just replace photon output with Photon Cannon.

blocks.speaker: Flu
blocks.content: Go ahead. I'll take it directly.
Flu's gaze was entirely different from when she evaluated Scatter Movement.
Photon Cannon, unlike photon output, was a complete attack spell. As this was the final evaluation, he had to give it his all.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone took a deep breath and timed his move. Assuming a one-second delay, he cast teleportation at 0.5 seconds to disrupt his opponent's tempo.
As his body glowed, Photon Cannons shot out in all directions, and Flu moved simultaneously.
Shirone arrived in the west. Therefore, the Photon Cannon heading towards Flu, who arrived in the south, was on target.
The Photon Cannon whizzed past her face and crashed into the wall with a thud, shaking the training ground.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: How was that? Will it work in real combat?
Flu reviewed the situation.
The pressure of receiving the Photon Cannon was much stronger than expected. The power came from its speed, which distinguished it from other spells.
Sub-light speed is a standard created to define the speed of light implemented by magic.
While not used in physics, it's a speed indistinguishable from light to the human eye.
If you shot an arrow with a light-emitting object at night, it would appear as a flash.
But experiencing the Photon Cannon was much faster than expected. It was closer to the speed of a bullet fired from a gun.
This meant the ratio of mass to output was perfectly balanced.
A larger mass than a magic bullet was propelled at bullet speed, causing the target to stiffen.
Even if the original Scatter Movement didn't hit the target, it would at most be a dud, but this was a bang.

blocks.speaker: Flu
blocks.content: Pass. This will definitely work in real combat.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Whew, thank goodness. The three weeks of hard work paid off. Thank you so much!
There was nothing more gratifying for a mage than learning a new spell, and Flu, understanding Shirone's feelings, replaced her strict expression with a smile.

blocks.speaker: Flu
blocks.content: You should maintain this feeling. Keep practicing if you want.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Of course. Remembering the sensation of success is important. Just don't overdo it.
From then on, Shirone practiced Scatter Movement until he was exhausted.
After two hours, he could move to any of the eight directions without any deviation in the power of the Photon Cannon.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Ouch, my back.
Flu, who had been watching Shirone's training, massaged her back, which had stiffened from dodging Photon Cannons.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: If she had dodged with physical strength, it might have been different, but moving with manipulation magic took its toll.
According to academic journals, Shirone's strength was his insight. He had an extreme tendency to delve into the core rather than accumulating miscellaneous skills.
This was likely what Gaold had high hopes for.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Whew.
Finishing his training, Shirone looked up at the ceiling, his face drenched in sweat.
The state of unity derived from a sense of achievement is a miraculous period where skills can improve rapidly.
Mages call this state 'fever.'

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: All done?

blocks.speaker: Flu
blocks.content: Yes, I think I've got the hang of it.

blocks.speaker: Flu
blocks.content: Starting tomorrow, we'll move on to Laser Guidance Technology. But there's something we need to address first.
The joy of success disappeared from Shirone's eyes.
He had to listen to whatever it was.
Living with the professional mages of the association, their mindset had naturally rubbed off on him.

blocks.speaker: Flu
blocks.content: Watching you for three weeks, I've noticed your balance is way off.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Huh? Balance?
Flu twirled her finger, roughly describing Shirone's Spirit Zone.

blocks.speaker: Flu
blocks.content: The biggest problem is that your Spirit Zone is too large. It feels like it's over 80 meters in diameter.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Is that a problem?
In real combat, the smallest details determine victory or defeat.
It's important to maintain the golden balance of density, volume, proportion, and durability.
You tend to overdo it. If your opponent's durability is 100, 100 power is enough. But you unconsciously pour in 120, 130.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Since the Spirit Zone is a manifestation of the mind, there's no lying.
Reflecting on all his past battles, Shirone realized this was true.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It's the same now. When our Spirit Zones overlap, the tension pricks at me. I've always wondered why. Unless you've only fought monsters.
Shirone couldn't deny it.
Looking back on the year since he started learning magic, he hadn't faced many equal opponents. By his standards, all his enemies were monsters.
As Shirone remained silent, Flu understood.

blocks.speaker: Flu
blocks.content: Anyway, that's not good. I'll help you find the golden balance.
Flu went to the wall and turned on the Image Zone switch.
As Shirone's Spirit Zone became visible with a bluish light, she turned off the training ground lights to enhance clarity.
Shirone watched his beautifully glowing Spirit Zone in silence.
At that moment, Flu hugged him from behind, pressing her body against his.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Uh, um...

blocks.speaker: Flu
blocks.content: Stay still.
Flu's calm voice stopped Shirone from speaking.
As she expanded her Spirit Zone, Shirone's Spirit Zone was clearly conveyed through shared perception.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: He's really tense. Almost like a mage in a war zone.

blocks.speaker: Flu
blocks.content: Close your eyes and take deep breaths. Reduce your Spirit Zone until you feel completely comfortable.
Shirone relaxed his body and let go of his tension.

blocks.speaker: Flu
blocks.content: Good... keep going...
As he reduced his Spirit Zone further, the tension he hadn't noticed before began to reveal itself.

blocks.speaker: Flu
blocks.content: More. More... that's it. Good.
At that point, Flu's voice faded away.
Shirone, with his head bowed, felt an overwhelming sense of warmth and peace, as if he were in his mother's embrace.

blocks.speaker: Flu
blocks.content: …Done?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Yes, it feels good.
Flu released her hands from Shirone's waist and quietly moved to the Image Zone device.
She checked the gauge, which read a diameter of 62.8 meters.
When the lights came on, Shirone opened his eyes, feeling refreshed as if he had just woken from a deep sleep.

blocks.speaker: Flu
blocks.content: Remember this feeling. The size, shape, sensation, density—everything. Everything contained within the 62.8-meter sphere is your current golden balance.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone understood Flu's words. The increased density made his surroundings feel clearer, and his durability felt significantly stronger.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Most importantly, his thoughts flowed smoothly, like water.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It feels really comfortable. But 62.8 meters isn't much larger than a year ago. Is that okay?

blocks.speaker: Flu
blocks.content: Yes, that's perfectly fine. Unless you're a sniper, you don't need an excessively large Spirit Zone. How often do you fight enemies at invisible distances? Even learning the four-directional technique doubles your range.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: That's true, but…

blocks.speaker: Flu
blocks.content: Trust me. For a search-type mage, a Spirit Zone diameter of at least a kilometer is necessary. Communication mages need an extended Spirit Zone to transmit information over tens of kilometers. But some combat mages have Spirit Zones only 10 meters in diameter, focusing instead on density and durability.
The key was to maintain a mental state that maximized the efficiency of his magic.
Don't be swayed by numbers. They can solidify your thinking. While gauging an opponent's capabilities by numbers is essential for combat mages, preemptively imagining the outcome is dangerous.

No trait is an absolute advantage or disadvantage. The criteria for advantage and disadvantage vary depending on the opponent, and your ability to adapt to various situations determines victory or defeat.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Yes, I'll keep that in mind.
